steps.explanation: Calculate the fixed cost for the first 25 people. At \$21 each: \(25 \times 21 = 525\) dollars. This amount is fixed for any group of 25 or more.
Calculate the variable cost for the additional people. For \(n \ge 25\), there are \(n - 25\) additional people beyond the first 25, each costing \$14. Variable cost: \(14(n-25)\).

Evaluate each choice.
  • Choice A: \(f(n) = 14n + 175\) — matches our derived formula exactly. This is the answer.
  • Choice B: \(f(n) = 14n + 525\) — uses the full fixed cost of 525 without subtracting \(14 \times 25 = 350\), double-counting the first 25 people at \$14 each. Eliminate B.
  • Choice C: \(f(n) = 35n - 350\) — uses 35 as the per-person rate, which does not match either \$21 or \$14. Eliminate C.
  • Choice D: \(f(n) = 14n + 21\) — the constant 21 is just the per-person rate for the first tier, not the total charge for 25 people. Eliminate D.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
